annotations.label: Genus (intuitive notion)

anchor.snippet: the genus is the number of

annotations.note: No topological notion of genus (number of handles) is defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus of sphere and torus

anchor.snippet: A sphere has genus 0, while a torus has genus 1

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of the genus of surfaces such as the sphere or torus.

annotations.label: Genus of an orientable surface

anchor.snippet: is an integer representing the maximum number of cuttings along non-intersecting closed simple curves

annotations.note: No classification or genus invariant of closed orientable surfaces exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus of sphere, disc, torus, mug

anchor.snippet: and a disc both have genus zero

annotations.note: No genus computations for specific surfaces appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-orientable genus

anchor.snippet: or Euler genus of a connected, non-orientable closed surface is a positive integer representing the number of cross-caps

annotations.note: Non-orientable genus and cross-caps are not formalized in Mathlib (a grep for crossCap/Klein bottle finds only the KleinFour group).

annotations.label: Non-orientable genus of projective plane and Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: A real projective plane has a non-orientable genus 1

annotations.note: Mathlib has no projective-plane or Klein-bottle surface with an associated genus.

annotations.label: Genus of a knot

anchor.snippet: is defined as the minimal genus of all Seifert surfaces

annotations.note: Mathlib contains no knot theory, Seifert surfaces, or knot genus.

annotations.label: Genus of a handlebody

anchor.snippet: is an integer representing the maximum number of cuttings along embedded disks

annotations.note: Handlebodies and their genus are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus of ball and solid torus

anchor.snippet: A ball has genus 0

annotations.note: No genus of balls or solid tori is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus of a graph

anchor.snippet: is the minimal integer n such that the graph can be drawn without crossing itself on a sphere with n handles

annotations.note: Mathlib has SimpleGraph but no notion of graph embedding genus or planarity-on-surfaces.

annotations.label: Non-orientable genus of a graph

anchor.snippet: The non-orientable genus of a graph is the minimal integer n such that the graph can be drawn without crossing itself on a sphere with n cross-caps

annotations.note: The non-orientable graph genus is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler genus of a graph

anchor.snippet: The Euler genus is the minimal integer n such that the graph can be drawn without crossing itself

annotations.note: Euler genus of graphs is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus of a group

anchor.snippet: The genus of a group G is the minimum genus of a (connected, undirected) Cayley graph

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Cayley graphs (Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Cayley) but no graph genus, so the genus of a group cannot be expressed.

annotations.label: Graph genus problem is NP-complete

anchor.snippet: The graph genus problem is NP-complete

annotations.note: Mathlib has no computational complexity theory (no NP-completeness framework) nor graph genus.

annotations.label: Arithmetic and geometric genus of a scheme

anchor.snippet: There are two related definitions of genus of any projective algebraic scheme

annotations.note: Neither arithmetic nor geometric genus of projective schemes is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Agreement of genus definitions for non-singular complex curves

anchor.snippet: then these definitions agree and coincide with the topological definition

annotations.note: No genus definitions exist in Mathlib, so their coincidence is not stated.

annotations.label: Elliptic curve as genus 1 curve

anchor.snippet: the definition of elliptic curve from algebraic geometry is connected non-singular projective curve of genus 1 with a given rational point on it

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes elliptic curves as Weierstrass curves with invertible discriminant (WeierstrassCurve.IsElliptic), not via the genus-1-with-rational-point characterization.

annotations.label: Genus formula via Riemann–Roch

anchor.snippet: By the Riemann–Roch theorem , an irreducible plane curve of degree

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch theorem for curves and no degree-genus formula.

annotations.label: Genus of an oriented manifold (cobordism)

anchor.snippet: a genus of an oriented manifold

annotations.note: Cobordism and genera in the sense of cobordism invariants are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus as ring homomorphism on cobordism ring

anchor.snippet: is a ring homomorphism

annotations.note: The cobordism ring and genus as a ring homomorphism are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elliptic genus

anchor.snippet: This genus is called an elliptic genus

annotations.note: Elliptic genera are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic is not a genus

anchor.snippet: is not a genus in this sense since it is not invariant concerning cobordisms

annotations.note: Mathlib has an algebraic Euler characteristic (HomologicalComplex.eulerChar) but no cobordism framework in which to state that it fails to be a genus.

annotations.label: Genus trace of biomolecules

anchor.snippet: Genus can be also calculated for the graph spanned by the net of chemical interactions

annotations.note: This biological application of graph genus is entirely outside Mathlib's scope.
